title: Allow to define the route for a job in the job metadata
body:
## In what area(s)?

/area runtime

## Describe the feature

I have a use case where I need to schedule jobs for videos that are uploaded and which are transcoded by a third party video provider. I need to schedule a job for each video. I can append the video ID to the job name itself.

```
sync-video-state-video-id
```

The route that is called for the job is:

```
/job/sync-video-state-video-id
```

This makes it hard to assign a single job handler to the router.

It would be great if I could have a route in the following format:

```
/job/sync-video-state/video-id
```

Therefore I suggest that the route that is called by the job can be defined when scheduling the job.

Using a route path as the job name itself is not possible:

```
sync-video-state/video-id
```

Results in:

```
a lowercase RFC 1123 subdomain must consist of lower case alphanumeric characters, '-' or '.', and must start and end with an alphanumeric character (e.g. 'example.com', regex used for validation is '[a-z0-9]([-a-z0-9]*[a-z0-9])?(\\.[a-z0-9]([-a-z0-9]*[a-z0-9])?)*')
```

Based on @cicoyle this is expected: That is part of how we namespace keys in etcd is why we don’t allow jobs with / in the name


## Release Note

RELEASE NOTE: **ADD** Allow to define the route that is called by a scheduled job.

comments.body: This makes sense to add. Thanks for the suggestion @akkie. The `override_route_path` seems very reasonable to me.

This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has not had activity in the last 60 days. It will be closed in the next 7 days unless it is tagged (pinned, good first issue, help wanted or triaged/resolved) or other activity occurs. Thank you for your contributions.
@cicoyle please can you tag the issue?
